entries.name: AI Dubbing: Transcription Proofreading
entries.description: Users can correct transcription errors and split or merge segments before dubbing. Preview the original video while reviewing the transcript, retranslate dubbed videos with one click after edits, and keep subtitles always in sync. Only edits matching the original voiceover are accepted, preserving meaning. Available in 97 languages.
entries.limitations: Retranslation affects the whole video and any edits to a translated video will be lost after re-syncing. Changing speaker assignment is not yet supported. Original transcript cannot be reached from the script box.

entries.name: Interactivity 2.0 (Quizzes)
entries.description: Add multiple-choice and single-choice questions directly inside Synthesia videos. Group them into quizzes and track scores. Question font supports an additional colour, and 'Practice mode' has been renamed to 'Scored'.
entries.limitations: Only 1 question component per scene and 1 results component per video. Interactive videos must be published via Synthesia for interactivity to work. SCORM completion criteria cannot be changed. Responses on non-scored questions aren't reported via SCORM.

entries.name: Customizable Express-2 Avatars (Outfits + Spaces)
entries.description: 6 new Express-2 stock avatars (Natasha, Hope, Carol, Clint, Bruce, Steve) where users can write a text prompt to create any outfit or space. Released to 100% of users including Freemium.
entries.limitations: Text-prompt support only (image upload coming later). Customization works only for these 6 avatars, not previous Express-1 or Express-2 avatars. Creating new outfits/spaces can take a minute or two.

entries.name: Avatar B-Roll (Avatars + Veo 3)
entries.description: Use the 6 customizable Express-2 avatars (with their outfits/spaces) for custom B-roll showing avatars doing actions and interacting with their environment, powered by Google Veo 3.
entries.limitations: B-roll works only for the 6 new customizable avatars. Default model Google Veo 3 costs 96 credits per generation. Google Veo 3 Fast available for 48 credits. Sora 2 not supported for avatars/spaces.

entries.name: Play Videos on Canvas
entries.description: Users can play video assets directly within the canvas without opening the scene. Works for videos with sound and trimmed videos. Playback controls adapt to element size and zoom level.
entries.limitations: Controls/timeline may be hidden when they don't fit. Playback stops when editing, rotating, or deselecting the element.

entries.name: AI Dubbing: 47 Additional Languages
entries.description: Expanded AI Dubbing from 32 to 79 languages and from 89 to 139 locales. More accurate accent performance for French (fr-FR, fr-CA) and Portuguese (pt-PT, pt-BR). Powered by an expanded voice cloning system using ElevenLabs and Microsoft Azure as providers.
entries.limitations: Users cannot choose the specific voice cloning provider themselves when dubbing.

entries.name: AI Dubbing: Improved Translation & Voiceover Speed
entries.description: Full sentences are no longer split into short phrases for dubbing. Sentences are translated and voiced over in context, producing more naturally sounding translations and more natural voiceover speed.
entries.limitations: Only affects newly uploaded videos. Translating a previously imported video uses the old approach.

entries.name: Voice Persistence
entries.description: The Voice Engine stores generated audio permanently at the paragraph level for each voice. Previews, regenerated videos, and duplicated videos use identical audio as long as the script is the same. Faster previews and fewer TTS provider calls.
entries.limitations: Users cannot yet re-generate or modify stored audio if they don't like it (addressed by Voice Regeneration). Workaround was modifying the script.

entries.name: Voice Regeneration
entries.description: If users are unhappy with a voice output, they can hit a regenerate button to get a new output without tweaking script punctuation. Undo restores the original output.
entries.limitations: No change to underlying voice models — regenerate just rolls the dice for non-deterministic models. Some voice models have less variance, so regenerated audio may sound similar. Works at paragraph level: regenerating any portion of a paragraph regenerates the whole paragraph.

entries.name: PowerPoint Import: Embedded Video Support
entries.description: Embedded videos in PPTX files are now imported into the editor and can be played from the canvas. File size limit raised to 1GB and slide support raised to 100 slides (from 250MB and 50 slides). Improved import-progress UX flow.

entries.name: AI Playground (formerly AI Clips)
entries.description: A dedicated tab where users can generate Media for their videos using models like Sora and Veo. Originally rolled out for AVG signups, then to 100% of Self-Service users, then to Enterprise. Includes an Explore tab with example content, prompt suggestions and shuffle, image creation, multi-asset generation, landscape and portrait aspect ratios, and Start/End Frame image upload support.
entries.limitations: Enterprise visibility governed by the existing 'AI-generated stock content' admin control. Start/End Frame is not available to Freemium users.

entries.name: ECS-Based Renderer (Thumbnails + Video)
entries.description: Thumbnails and videos are generated by a new Entity-Component-System renderer. Faster generation, fewer failures, and foundations for richer animations. 100% of thumbnails and ramping to 100% of videos externally.

entries.name: Scene & Video Issue Indicators
entries.description: Validation indicators show errors in scenes and videos as they appear or are fixed, before clicking Generate. Users can spot scenes with errors directly in the left scene list panel. Validation runs reactively for faster UI when generating complex videos.
entries.limitations: Some issues auto-fix when clicking Generate, which could cause confusion since they're now visible. Reactive validation may slow UI in some cases.

entries.name: Personal Avatars from Photo
entries.description: Create a realistic personal avatar from a single photo within minutes (versus a day for video-based avatars). Built on Express-2 with lifelike body language synced to the script, full-body framing including hands. Customize outfit, background, and use for B-roll powered by Google Veo 3. Released alongside the legacy video-based Personal Avatar flow, not replacing it.
entries.limitations: Avatar movements are not based on real-life movement (single photo input). Changing outfit or background may slightly affect avatar likeness. Lip sync works best when avatar is closer to the camera. Cannot share these Personal Avatars with other users yet. Starter: 3 personal avatars, Creator: 5, Enterprise: unlimited (shared cap across photo and video methods).

entries.name: Dubbing API
entries.description: Programmatically dub videos via API, enabling automation, bulk operations, and integrations through plugins (e.g., Synthesia Microsoft Excel Plugin). Replaces single-video dubbing through the web UI for at-scale workflows.
tiers: All plans with standard API access
entries.limitations: Standard API rate limits for write operations apply.

entries.name: 7 French (France) Stock Voices via Azure
entries.description: Added 7 new French stock voices (Ellie - Composed, Melody - Smooth, Francesca/Donna - Assured, Arthur - Informative, Ryan - Steady, Jaz - Casual, Michael - Natural). Use Azure as a provider rather than ElevenLabs because Azure better preserves the France accent.

entries.name: Review Transcript Before Dubbing
entries.description: The Dubbing Modal now offers two options: Review Transcript and Dub. Selecting Review Transcript transcribes the video and lets users proofread and fix transcription errors without spending credits upfront. UI improved: a translate button appears at the top right corner of the video editor for both translation and dubbing.

entries.name: AI Image Generation (FLUX.2 + Nano Banana 2)
entries.description: Generate images with AI inside the Media panel by writing a text prompt or pressing TAB. Two models: FLUX.2 by Black Forest Labs (3 credits/image self-serve, FREE for Enterprise) and Google Nano Banana 2 (9 credits/image all plans). Image generation is 10x cheaper than video generation.
entries.limitations: Only landscape 1080p resolution supported. Not integrated with Avatars and Spaces the way Veo 3.1 is. Results may vary (misspelled text, weird objects).

entries.name: Scene Limit Increased to 150
entries.description: Scene limit increased from 50 to 150 (3x). Total audio length cap of ~4 hours per video. Max 5 minutes audio per scene. Customers can build a video with 150 short scenes or 50 longer scenes, or any combination.
entries.limitations: Max 150 scenes; max 5 minutes audio per scene; max ~4 hours total audio per video.

entries.name: Preview Any Script Selection
entries.description: Users can preview any selection of their script audio. Previously the system auto-expanded selection to the whole sentence (later paragraph), forcing users to listen to more than they wanted.

entries.name: Express-1 Stock Avatar Drop (Nov 2025)
entries.description: 8 new Express-1 stock avatars added: Sydney, Orla, Lizzie, Nia, Christian (2 looks) — available in close, wide, and side-angle framings; Tamara, Trinity, Jackie — available in close and wide framings.

entries.name: SCIM (User Lifecycle Automation)
entries.description: SCIM is generally available for automating user lifecycle management at scale, reducing IT overhead and enabling Enterprise customers to securely scale Synthesia usage.
entries.limitations: SSO must be set up again with WorkOS before setting up SCIM. Customers must be on an ORG.

entries.name: Self-Serve Email Change
entries.description: Users can change their sign-in email from their profile view without contacting Customer Success. Process is verified and user-initiated.
entries.limitations: Limited to users who created accounts using the email/password sign-up method (leverages AWS Cognito for security).

entries.name: PPT-to-Video Onboarding (Desktop)
entries.description: Desktop users landing on the PPT-to-video page now go directly into the Video Editor with their PowerPoint file imported, all assets and speaker notes extracted. Mobile users continue to get the Assistant-based flow.
entries.limitations: Desktop only.

entries.name: Courses (Closed Beta)
entries.description: Organize videos into a cohesive learning experience and track learner progress. Includes Course Builder, Copilot AI Assistant for outline/script generation, Storyboard view, Skills tagging, Course Player with progress tracking, Course Sharing (share page, embed, SCORM), and Course Analytics with skill distribution charts and lesson drop-off charts.
entries.limitations: Copilot doesn't support agent/visual creation. Lesson videos must be generated one by one. Reverting to a different lesson version requires restoring from editor and regenerating. Lessons can be watched in any order. Learner names tracked only with SSO or via SCORM. Translations not yet supported. Excludes Agents.

entries.name: Automatic Video Retry on Failures
entries.description: Videos automatically retry when they encounter temporary issues (API slowdowns, rate limits, brief service hiccups), with adjusted spacing between attempts that can handle multi-hour service disruptions. Users have clear visibility when their videos are retrying.
entries.limitations: Longer time to failure for non-transient issues.

entries.name: Santa Avatars (Express-2)
entries.description: Two Santa avatars (Classic Santa in a cozy room with Mrs. Santa portrait; Working Santa with a 'Boss' mug) created from pictures using Express-2 tech with fully generative gestures. Default UK Santa voice with US and 70+ other voice options. Templates designed for both avatars.

entries.name: PT/FR Accent Selection for Voice Clones & Personal Avatars
entries.description: Enterprise users can select a Portuguese or French accent as their base language when creating a voice clone or personal avatar, retaining the speaker's selected accent.
entries.limitations: Non-Enterprise users continue to see only 'French' and 'Portuguese' as before.

entries.name: Dubbing Tab
entries.description: A dedicated Dubbing tab is now live in the left navigation for both Self-Service and Enterprise users. Reduced low-intent workflow entries without regression in dubbed video completions.
entries.limitations: Enterprise visibility governed by the existing 'AI Dubbing' setting. If disabled, users won't see the tab in the left-nav.

entries.name: Voice Speed Controls
entries.description: Set a voice's speed as low as 0.8x and as high as 1.2x. Adjust the voice per paragraph and optionally across the whole video.
entries.limitations: Most but not all voices supported (Synthesia, ElevenLabs, Microsoft Azure, Google, Watson). Numeric controls are approximate (1.2x is not exactly 20% faster).

entries.name: Synthesia Excel Add-In: Bulk Dubbing
entries.description: Upload existing MP4, MOV, WEBM files into Excel and dub them into one or more languages at scale. Choose source/target languages, lip-sync preference, and run hundreds of dubbing jobs in one go.
entries.limitations: In some orgs, IT admin approval may be required to install the Excel add-in.

entries.name: Storyboard View in Editor
entries.description: A new script-focused view in the Editor that shows your entire video script in one continuous, scrollable layout. Open via the expand icon in the script box. Includes Layout Replacement (AI-powered) which uses the Assisted Creation population endpoint to take a scene's content and fill a template, giving access to all templates.
entries.limitations: Can copy script out of Storyboard but not paste a fully edited script back in. Highlighting across multiple scenes not supported. Drag-and-drop scene reordering not yet available. Coming soon: Copilot integration in Storyboard.

entries.name: 7 French ElevenLabs Voices
entries.description: Added 7 additional French (France) voices via ElevenLabs (Claire variants: Conversational, Lively, Instructional; plus male voices: Lively, Serious, Instructional, Calm). Available to all users.

entries.name: Mobile AVG/Dubbing Email Flow
entries.description: Mobile users signing up via web tools now receive an email notification when their video is ready, instead of being shown a holding page. Emails route users by device type — desktop users go into product, mobile users see their video on the marketing site.

entries.name: Haitian Creole Language Support
entries.description: Haitian Creole is supported with 4 stock voices (2 female, 2 male; named 'Professional' and 'Welcoming'). Differentiator vs competitors who don't support this language.
entries.limitations: Translating into Haitian Creole only available via the new LLM translation engine (currently in beta). Not available for voice cloning or dubbing output.

entries.name: Avatar Video Persistence
entries.description: Lock in and reuse previously generated avatar videos as long as you use the same audio + avatar on a given scene. Faster video generation, lower compute costs, and improved consistency when regenerating videos.
entries.limitations: To force an avatar to regenerate, you must change the audio (via script edit or Voice Regeneration). Avatar performances are not persisted across workspaces. Estimated generation times don't account for this yet.

entries.name: Branding for Avatar Customization (Logos + Colors)
entries.description: Customize an avatar's outfit or space with a logo from your Brand Kit/Library or upload a new one. Use a hex code to choose a specific color or let the system suggest a prompt using your Brand Kit's primary color. Updated underlying model produces sharper outputs that follow prompts more faithfully.
entries.limitations: Only works for the 6 Avatars that support customization. Avatar appearance may look slightly different than before due to new model. Logos may subtly change when added; highly detailed or small-text logos are hardest to render accurately.

entries.name: Editable Rejected Videos
entries.description: Rejected videos remain editable and can be resubmitted instead of being marked as deleted. Users can fix moderation issues and resubmit directly without starting over.

entries.name: Freemium Plan: 10 Minutes / 1,200 Credits Monthly
entries.description: Freemium plan increased from 3 minutes (360 credits) to 10 minutes (1,200 credits) monthly to provide a richer experience for free users and support repeat usage. Existing freemium users migrated in batches.

entries.name: LLM-Driven Translation Engine
entries.description: New centralised, platform-level microservice that delivers high-quality, context-aware translations for synthetic videos using LLMs. Replaces DeepL + Google Translate for synthetic video translation. Better locale-specific nuances (e.g. Spanish (Mexico) vs (Colombia) vs (Spain)).
entries.limitations: Customers who opted out of LLMs cannot use the new engine. Japanese translations fall back to DeepL until formality controls launch. Courses don't yet use LLM translations.
supersedes: DeepL/Google Translate as the synthetic-video translation default

entries.name: Extended Locales for Translations (160+)
entries.description: Translation support expanded from 80+ to 160+ locales. Region-specific translations like French (Canada), Spanish (Mexico), Arabic (Qatar) with locale-appropriate accented voices.
entries.limitations: Falls back to legacy translation flow (DeepL + Google Translate) on errors; locale reverts to base language code (e.g. 'es-mx' -> 'es').

entries.name: Translation Glossary (GA)
entries.description: Workspace-level glossary that ensures company, product, and industry terms are translated consistently or preserved as-is across synthetic video translations. Glossary editing permissions controlled in workspace settings.
entries.limitations: One glossary per workspace (downloadable as CSV for use in other workspaces). Glossary rules apply only to newly created translations. Not available to customers who opted out of LLM translations. Not supported for Dubbing yet (planned Q2 2026). CSV imports replace existing rules; no in-product conflict resolution. Contextual understanding may not always disambiguate terms with multiple meanings.

entries.name: Script Remediation Guidance (GA)
entries.description: Real-time script feedback when users open the generate panel — identifies and fixes script moderation issues before generation. Reported a ~59% reduction in auto-rejections (self-serve & freemium) and a 2.39% increase in video creation.
entries.limitations: Self-serve/Freemium: always on. Enterprise: off by default — admins control enablement via workspace or org settings.

entries.name: Voice Deprecations (Feb 2026)
entries.description: A small set of voices were removed from the Synthesia voice library by end of February 2026 because the underlying provider deprecated them. Recommended replacements: Considered→Gravelly, Balanced→Chill, Dynamic→Chill, Measured→Firm, Vibrant→Measured, Gentle→Composed, Bright→Sincere, Mellow→Measured, Energetic→Firm, Determined→Measured.
entries.limitations: Voices only deprecated in select languages.

entries.name: Audit Logs
entries.description: Organisation admins can track and monitor activity across their Synthesia organisation. View activity history, filter by date/user/event type, export to CSV, and access audit logs via API. Acting User column shows the real person who initiated billing credit-consuming actions (no longer 'System').
entries.limitations: Requires Organisation Admin permissions. Background/system-initiated jobs still show 'System'. Past events show 'System' (audit events are immutable; not backfilled).

entries.name: Share.synthesia.io Pages Set to Noindex
entries.description: The 'indexable share page' feature was removed. share.synthesia.io pages are no longer crawlable by search engines (noindex applied). Studio no longer shows toggles for switching page indexability when publishing a video or course.

entries.name: Express-2 Avatars High-Res Rendering
entries.description: 100% of Express-2 avatars are now served via the higher-quality high-res rendering route. Sharper, more realistic avatars; correlates with higher publish/utilisation rates.
entries.limitations: Initial rollout via FAL was slow (doubled/tripled latency); team progressing on hosting model in-house.

entries.name: Gradient on Text
entries.description: Add a gradient between two colors as a text background instead of just a single color.
entries.limitations: Gradient color cannot be combined with other text effects (text stroke, underline, shadows).

entries.name: Folder Sharing Settings
entries.description: Share folders and collaborate with specific users. Permission levels: General Access at workspace (No access, Commenter, Editor, Full Access) and User-invited (Commenter, Editor, Full Access). Shared folders appear in 'Shared with me'. Permissions propagate from parent folder → subfolders → files unless manually overridden.
entries.limitations: Tier B Enterprise customers receive a 2-week delay rollout.

entries.name: Bulk Replace/Delete Images on Canvas
entries.description: When replacing or deleting an image, users are asked whether they'd like to apply the change to all matching images across the video. Replace works via Replace image button or paste; remove works only on delete (not cut).
entries.limitations: Only enabled for images, not videos.

entries.name: Environments Tab Retired
entries.description: The Environments tab in the media picker was retired. Analytics showed the feature was very rarely used; removal reduces clutter in the picker.

entries.name: Improved Avatar Replacement Logic
entries.description: When replacing a multicam avatar (single or bulk), the previous avatar's Camera angle is preserved. When replacing outfits or spaces, they're replaced independently — replacing an outfit retains the existing space (and vice versa). New scenes added via template show the outfit combined with the template space.

entries.name: Selfie Avatars Retirement (June 1, 2026)
entries.description: Selfie Avatars will be retired on June 1, 2026 (90-day notice). Replaced by Personal Avatars from Photo, which addresses the same user needs with more capabilities (outfits, spaces, B-roll). Selfie Avatar beta is closed and no longer accepting sign-ups.
entries.limitations: After June 1, users cannot create new Selfie Avatars. Existing Selfie Avatars accessible only if explicitly saved via 'Save to My Avatars' button. Unsaved avatars no longer accessible after that date.

entries.name: Personal Avatar Creation Limits
entries.description: Plan-based limits on Personal Avatar creation: Enterprise users — unlimited (photo or video). Creator plan — 5 Personal Avatars (shared cap across photo and video). Starter plan — 3 Personal Avatars (shared cap). Any user can customize a Personal Avatar from a Photo into unlimited outfits.
entries.limitations: Caps are shared between photo-based and video-based creation methods.
supersedes: earlier statement of unlimited photo-based personal avatars

entries.name: Voice Runtime Triton (VoRT) for Express-Voice
entries.description: Express-voice voice clone generation and TTS now run on Voice Runtime Triton, a new architecture similar to Avatar Runtime Triton (ART). Enables more efficient operation, easier deployment of updates, and running multiple models simultaneously.
entries.limitations: Watching for TTS latency and voice clone generation errors.

entries.name: Express-Voice Underlying Model Updated (IPA Foundation)
entries.description: The underlying voice model powering all Express voices (including English Express-voice voice clones) was replaced. Prepares the foundation for upcoming IPA pronunciation capabilities. Sounds extremely similar to the old model — no audible difference expected.
entries.limitations: IPA pronunciation controls not yet enabled in product.

entries.name: Synthesia for Mobile
entries.description: Mobile experience rolled out to 100% of self-serve and freemium users as the new baseline. Covers sign-up, onboarding, content consumption, and media generation via Playground and Dubbing. Increased early engagement: returning users +36%, average first-24h session duration +66%.
entries.limitations: Video editor and Copilot/Assistant out of scope for MVP. Personal avatar creation, video assistant v2, and Enterprise functionality coming next.

entries.name: Personal Avatars New Creation Experience
entries.description: Wizard-like creation flow for Personal Avatars with more context and visuals at each step. Studio Avatars moved to top-right of first screen. 'Request a Personal Avatar' lives on the second screen after selecting 'Personal Avatar'. Avatar Builder remains hidden.
entries.limitations: Removed option to upload audio to create a voice (low usage). Voice may not be ready immediately if a video is created very quickly after avatar creation.

entries.name: AI Playground: Start/End Frame Image Uploads
entries.description: Upload an image and use it as a starting frame, or as both start and end frames, alongside a text prompt to guide video generation. Supports PNG, JPG, WebP.
entries.limitations: Freemium users see an upgrade prompt.

entries.name: Button & Menu Elements + Surfaced Interactivity in Editor
entries.description: Added Button and Menu as ready-to-use elements directly in the Editor. Surfaced Interactivity and Dynamic Captions in the Editor too. Both interactive elements and Dynamic Captions automatically pick up Brand Kit colours and fonts.
entries.limitations: Menus currently support 2 options per Menu element (use copy + paste to add more). Interactivity available on Creator and Enterprise plans.

entries.name: AI Screen Recorder: Improved Transcription
entries.description: Record in any language and get an accurate transcript without manually selecting language upfront. Better pause detection improves scene-creation timestamp quality. Works from Studio ('Upload Screen Recording') or the browser extension.
entries.limitations: If detection is ambiguous (silent or non-speech audio), a manual language picker appears as fallback.

entries.name: Upload Screen Recording
entries.description: Upload an MP4 of a screen recording from Camtasia, QuickTime, Loom, or any tool — get a transcribed, editable Synthesia video with scenes automatically created from the content. Voiceover from SMEs is transcribed.
entries.limitations: Coming soon: upload an MP4 from the homepage to start a new video directly.

entries.name: Audio Graph in Editor Preview
entries.description: When an actor is speaking in editor preview playback, background music and video elements automatically get quieter — matching the final rendered video.
entries.limitations: Disabling enableAudioGraph feature flag is the first thing to try for audio issues.

entries.name: Transparent Background Avatars (Express-2)
entries.description: Remove the background from Express-2 avatars (both Stock and Personal) and use them on top of other canvas elements. Add solid color or gradient backgrounds to an avatar. Released to 100% of users by 26 March 2026.
entries.limitations: In some cases, fuzziness along avatar edges due to incomplete green-screen removal. Rendering times may be ~7% longer. Oldest Express-2 avatars not supported (Ada, Ryan, Zola, Michael, Joshua, Ellie, Santa).

entries.name: Remastered Express-2 Avatars (Fabulous 6)
entries.description: Base images of the 6 customizable stock avatars (Natasha, Hope, Carol, Clint, Bruce, Steve) refreshed for sharper, more realistic skin, lighting, and picture quality.
entries.limitations: Old images of these avatars (with default outfits in stock spaces) are no longer accessible and replaced with new images. Custom outfit/space images remain unchanged. Doesn't address lip sync, facial expressions, or body language.

entries.name: Sora 2 Retirement (Sept 24, 2026)
entries.description: Sora 2 will be retired on September 24, 2026. OpenAI is shutting down their Sora video platform. After removal, users will still have Google Veo 3.1 and Veo 3.1 Fast. Product team plans to add optional audio for Veo models before Sora retirement (Veo with audio costs 2x credits).
entries.limitations: Currently no in-product mention that Sora is retiring; will be added before Sept 24.

entries.name: Fabulous 4 Customizable Avatars
entries.description: 4 brand-new Express-2 avatars: Claire, Daniel, Sarah, and Luke. Available in the Editor and paired in Assistant with a motion graphic style.
entries.limitations: Paired with existing voices (no new voices). Voice/lip sync/facial expressions/body language follow the current Express-2 avatar system.

entries.name: Synthesia Assistant (GA)
entries.description: AI-powered creation tool that produces higher-quality scripts with matching visuals in a video-first style using a new script-writing framework. Uses stock styles to generate consistent visuals across motion graphics, avatars, B-roll, and images. Accepts files, URLs, and prompts as context. Supports chat-based iteration inside the Editor. Ships alongside (not replacing) the legacy AI Video Assistant.
entries.limitations: Only supports one template (Assistant Default) at GA. No support for brand kits yet, just stock styles. Chat-based editing only works for Assistant-created videos. Asset generation can take a few minutes. Visual quality (motion graphics, B-roll) still WIP. Credit-consuming actions (like Veo3 b-roll) are free for the foreseeable future.

entries.name: Motion Graphics in Editor (Beta)
entries.description: Generate motion assets directly inside a scene based on the script using pre-baked styles. Adds sound effects. Works especially well for cards, timelines, and checklists. Free during beta.
entries.limitations: Quality can be inconsistent. Cannot brand or edit these assets. Generation limited to 30 seconds max. Users with AI Media turned off will not see this feature.

entries.name: Smooth Voice Generation
entries.description: Script audio is now generated up to entire paragraphs at a time instead of sentence by sentence. More natural, less choppy voice output with stable volume and tone. Quality matches generating directly on ElevenLabs.
entries.limitations: Not all providers support this — supported on ElevenLabs, Azure, and Synthesia's Express-Voice. Long paragraphs may take slightly longer to preview. Voices may sound slightly faster (no fixed pause after every sentence).

entries.name: Rendered Video Timing Accuracy Fix
entries.description: Rendered videos now have more accurate timing throughout, with cleaner stitching between parts created during rendering. Improves technical quality and reduces timing-related issues for downstream systems.
entries.limitations: Some exported videos may be slightly larger in file size as a tradeoff.

entries.name: Auto-Generate + Copy Share Link in One Click
entries.description: Single-click 'Copy link' replaces the previous two-step 'Create link' → copy flow. Generates the share link and copies it to clipboard in the same action. Rolled out to 100% of Freemium, Self-Serve, and Enterprise users.

entries.name: Sharing Benefits Modal on Download
entries.description: When users trigger a video download, a modal appears highlighting sharing benefits (the download still starts automatically). Users can copy a share link or dismiss the modal (won't reappear once dismissed). Now baseline for Self-Serve Paid & Enterprise users.

entries.name: Voice Recommendations Improvements
entries.description: Three improvements: (1) Bad voices removed from 120+ stock templates. (2) Bug in AI onboarding fixed — no longer defaults to 'en-GB Original' voice. (3) Translating into Japanese now uses native Japanese voices instead of trying to maintain the avatar's voice (which sounded non-native).

entries.name: Updated Trial Experience
entries.description: Trialists can end their trial early and immediately start their paid subscription. Trial credits are pro-rated, so starting earlier gives access to a full credit allowance. Video downloading is restricted during the trial period.
entries.limitations: USA only — trials not currently available in any other geos.

entries.name: Contextual Upgrade Plan Picker
entries.description: In-product plan picker now focuses the experience on upgrade paths based on the user's current subscription. Rolled out as baseline for self-service paid users.

entries.name: Onboarding Auto-Advance
entries.description: Onboarding questionnaire automatically advances users to the next question as soon as they select a response. Standardised modal sizing, consistent chip button styling, skip option for the company URL step. Now baseline for both Self-Service and Enterprise users.

entries.name: Per-Corner Border Radius
entries.description: Set individual border radii for each corner of rectangular base elements (images, rectangle shapes, videos, SVG icons). Click a button on any rectangular element to toggle per-corner mode.
entries.limitations: Does not support elements other than base rectangular shapes with 4 corners.

entries.name: Pause/Restart Control for Blocking Branches
entries.description: Users can pause the video using on-screen controls during blocking branches in the player. Previously this was only possible via keyboard shortcuts. The video control is now responsible only for restarting the played segment of a blocking branch (quizzes have their own Reply button).
entries.limitations: Scrubbing capability for blocking branches not yet available.

entries.name: Feature Highlights While Waiting
entries.description: During video generation, the 'video is generating' animation is being replaced with feature highlight cards. Cards are dynamic by plan with CTAs shown/hidden based on feature availability. Currently in 50/50 A/B test for desktop users across all plans.
entries.limitations: Desktop only. Currently in A/B test, not rolled out as baseline.

entries.name: Resize and Typing Performance Improvements
entries.description: Resize operations and typing in text elements deliver up to 3x more FPS during resize and no noticeable lag while typing. Especially helpful in large videos with many elements.

entries.name: 52 Weeks of Fixes (Ongoing UI Polish Programme)
entries.description: Ongoing programme of small UI/UX improvements. Highlights include: True Lock (locked objects can't be nudged with arrow keys), Homepage copy refresh (clearer New video / Translate / Import PowerPoint actions), Video title in browser tab, Aligned template cards, Community link in Help menu, Smarter rounded corners on shapes, Improved large uploads (>250MB more stable), Open videos in new tab, Higher-contrast active scene highlight, Clearer delete confirmations, More tooltips across the UI.
